story_clusters.summary: Iran has stated it will close the Strait of Hormuz, accusing the US and Israel of violating a ceasefire deal. The Strait of Hormuz is a strategic waterway connecting the Persian Gulf to the Gulf of Oman, through which a significant portion of the world's oil is transported. This move has implications for countries reliant on oil imports, including India.

story_clusters.summary: Shashi Tharoor expressed support for a meeting between Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and US President Donald Trump at the G7 summit. This statement led to criticism from other Congress leaders, including Pawan Khera, who made a remark about Tharoor calling Modi "Maha-Maanav" (a term of endearment). The exchange has reignited a rift within the Congress party.

articles.title: Modi: Yoga Connects All, A Must For Global Peace, Better Future Of The World
articles.summary: The PM observed, “In India, we have seen it as an expression of the human spirit with consciousness. That is why this year's theme has been made: Yoga for healthy aging. We can stay healthy, active and youthful despite aging. Yoga shows us the way for this. When we speak for Yoga for healthy aging, it means that we can work to ensure Age does not reduce human potential. Yoga can help human life to aspire for constant growth”

articles.title: India's data centre pipeline hits 8.33 GW as AI demand reshapes digital infra: Knight Frank India
articles.summary: India's data centre sector is witnessing an unprecedented infrastructure build-up, with the total development pipeline across major markets reaching 8.33 GW, according to Knight Frank India. articles.title: Magistrate Can't Reject Negative Final Report Solely Based On Protest Petition: Rajasthan High Court Quashes Cognizance In Rape Case
articles.summary: The Rajasthan High Court has held that a Magistrate cannot reject a negative final report and take cognizance of offences solely on the basis of the averments made in a protest petition, without meaningfully considering the material collected during investigation and recording reasons for disagreeing with the investigating officer's conclusions. Holding that the cognizance order suffered from non-application of mind, Justice Farjand Ali quashed the proceedings against two accused in a rape case

articles.title: NRE Account Credits Cannot Be Taxed Without Proof They Are Unexplained: ITAT Ahmedabad
articles.summary: The ITAT in Ahmedabad deleted an addition relating to an NRE account after finding that the credits were linked to overseas salary remittances and lacked evidence of unexplained income. articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) in Ahmedabad ordered the rectification of Ambuja Cements' share register due to a fraudulent transfer. This decision aims to restore the original shareholders' names and rectify the company's Register of Members.

articles.title: SC Upholds Dismissal as Employee Had No Right to Representative from Another Bank’s Union
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld the dismissal of an employee who was denied representation by a union official from another bank. The court ruled that the Service Code permits representation only through a union or association of the Bank of Cochin's employees or with permission by a lawyer. This decision has significant implications for employment law in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that the Service Code permitted representation only through a union or association of the Bank of Cochin's employees or, with permission, by a lawyer. Denial of representation by an office-bearer of another bank's union did not invalidate the disciplinary proceedings. The post SC Upholds Dismissal as Employee Had No Right to Representative from Another Bank s Union appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: IRDAI Proposes Major Actuarial Rule Changes to Align with Insurance Law Amendments
articles.summary: IRDAI has proposed major changes to actuarial rules to align with the Insurance Law Amendments. This move aims to overhaul actuarial governance, reporting, investment norms, and insurer compliance. The changes are expected to impact the insurance sector in India.
articles.raw_summary: IRDAI's draft 2026 amendments overhaul actuarial governance, reporting, investment norms, and insurer compliance to align with the Sabka Bima Sabki Raksha (Amendment of Insurance Laws) Act, 2025. The post IRDAI Proposes Major Actuarial Rule Changes to Align with Insurance Law Amendments appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Section 69C Addition Deleted as Diary Entries Alone cannot Prove Unexplained Expenditure
articles.summary: ITAT Chennai held that loose sheets and estimates alone cannot justify an addition under Section 69B without independent corroborative evidence. The Tribunal deleted the addition after finding no proof of investment outside the books of account. The post Section 69C Addition Deleted as Diary Entries Alone cannot Prove Unexplained Expenditure appeared first on TaxGuru .
articles.raw_summary: ITAT Chennai held that loose sheets and estimates alone cannot justify an addition under Section 69B without independent corroborative evidence. The Tribunal deleted the addition after finding no proof of investment outside the books of account. articles.title: ITAT Deletes Unexplained Investment Addition as Investments Were Already in Balance Sheet
articles.summary: The ITAT Nagpur deleted an addition of unexplained investments after finding that the Assessing Officer had added disclosed investments. This decision affects taxpayers in India. The ITAT's ruling is a significant development in tax law.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT Nagpur held that investments already disclosed in the balance sheet could not be treated as unexplained investments. It deleted the addition after finding that the Assessing Officer had added disclosed investments. Investors will closely watch its ability to improve monetisation and profitability while maintaining growth.

articles.title: Internet now a lifeline, focus should shift from popularising access to protecting users: IT Secretary
articles.summary: The Indian government is shifting its focus from promoting internet access to protecting users and ensuring robust internet infrastructure. IT Secretary S Krishnan emphasized the need for a multi-stakeholder approach to internet governance. This move aims to ensure the safety and security of internet users in India.
articles.raw_summary: The government's digital policy is shifting from promoting access to actively protecting users and ensuring robust internet infrastructure, IT Secretary S Krishnan announced. He emphasised the need for a multi-stakeholder approach to internet governance, involving academia, users, and private firms.

articles.title: Exports up 15% despite global headwinds: Commerce and industry minister Piyush Goyal
articles.summary: India's merchandise exports saw a robust 15% year-on-year growth in April-June, defying global economic headwinds, according to Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal. This positive trend continues despite US tariff impositions. Meanwhile, crucial trade talks with the US are set to commence this week, aiming to finalize a bilateral trade agreement framework. Discussions will address tariffs and potential new probes.

articles.title: India initiates anti-dumping probe against a Chinese, Japanese chemical used in tyre, rubber items
articles.summary: India has launched an anti-dumping investigation into Resorcinol imports from China and Japan. This action follows a complaint alleging that cheap imports are harming the domestic industry. The Directorate General of Trade Remedies will determine if dumping has occurred and caused injury. If confirmed, duties may be recommended to ensure fair trade practices for Indian producers.

If confirmed, duties may be recommended to ensure fair trade practices for Indian producers.

articles.title: AI startups fuel talent war as hiring surges across India
articles.summary: India's AI startups are experiencing a hiring surge, outpacing the broader tech sector as companies transition from AI experiments to large-scale deployments. Driven by demand for generative AI and automation, recruitment is booming across engineering, product, and customer-facing roles. This growth, however, highlights a significant talent shortage, pushing compensation for experienced AI professionals to new heights, with leadership roles commanding substantial packages.

articles.title: RBI MPC minutes: Policy misstep fears drove rate pause amid West Asia uncertainty
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee has opted for a cautious "wait and watch" stance, holding the repo rate steady due to significant geopolitical and weather-related uncertainties. Concerns over rising crude oil prices and potential El Nino impacts are driving inflation worries. While core inflation remains subdued, the committee emphasized vigilance against generalized price increases, prioritizing data dependency before considering any policy rate adjustments.

While core inflation remains subdued, the committee emphasized vigilance against generalized price increases, prioritizing data dependency before considering any policy rate adjustments.

articles.title: RBI MPC minutes: 'Energy prices unlikely to return to pre-conflict levels,' says economist Saugata Bhattacharya
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee maintained the repo rate at 5.25% with a neutral stance. External member Saugata Bhattacharya highlighted escalating inflationary risks from supply shocks, weather patterns, and energy prices. He also noted rising inflation expectations and potential global spillovers. Despite these concerns, he advocated for the status quo, citing tight domestic financial conditions and the need for a risk management approach amid prevailing uncertainties.

articles.title: Gameskraft founders' ED arrest illegal, Karnataka High Court orders immediate release
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court has ruled that the Enforcement Directorate’s arrest of Gameskraft founders Deepak Singh, Vikas Taneja and Prithviraj Singh was illegal and ordered their immediate release. The founders were arrested in May in connection with a money laundering probe linked to online gaming FIRs, with the ED alleging irregular transactions through Gameskraft’s real-money gaming platforms.

articles.title: Why India, among largest economies, doesn’t rank in top 50 world’s most powerful passports
articles.summary: Despite India's robust economic growth, its passport ranks surprisingly low globally, hovering around the 80th position. This reflects a slower expansion of visa-free access compared to other nations. Factors like diplomatic ties, reciprocal agreements, and perceived security influence rankings, not just economic might. India's progress is gradual, requiring strategic diplomatic efforts and strengthened internal systems to boost international trust and improve its travel freedom.

articles.title: 7 new bullet train corridors approved: Centre steps up high-speed rail expansion across India
articles.summary: India is set to revolutionize its rail network with the approval of seven new bullet train corridors, promising significantly reduced travel times between major cities. Key routes like Mumbai-Ahmedabad will see journeys slashed to under two hours, while Bengaluru-Chennai will take just over an hour. This ambitious expansion, including the Delhi-Varanasi-Patna corridor, aims to boost connectivity and usher in an era of unprecedented railway development across the nation.

This ambitious expansion, including the Delhi-Varanasi-Patna corridor, aims to boost connectivity and usher in an era of unprecedented railway development across the nation.

articles.title: RBI revises Kisan Credit Card norms, standardises crop season definition
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank has revamped the Kisan Credit Card (KCC) Scheme, standardizing crop season definitions for uniform loan sanctions and repayments. Effective January, new directions aim to streamline credit support for agriculture and allied activities. Banks must waive collateral for loans up to Rs 2 lakh, with exceptions for voluntary gold/silver pledges. For loans exceeding Rs 2 lakh, banks will set collateral based on their policy.
articles.raw_summary: The Reserve Bank has revamped the Kisan Credit Card (KCC) Scheme, standardizing crop season definitions for uniform loan sanctions and repayments. Effective January, new directions aim to streamline credit support for agriculture and allied activities. Banks must waive collateral for loans up to Rs 2 lakh, with exceptions for voluntary gold/silver pledges. For loans exceeding Rs 2 lakh, banks will set collateral based on their policy.

articles.title: India initiates sustainability certification framework for exports to meet EU, UK compliance norms
articles.summary: India is creating new sustainability certification standards for its export products. This initiative aims to boost the competitiveness of Indian goods in international markets. Developed nations are increasingly demanding proof of sustainability. The commerce and industry ministry is gathering information from various export sectors. This move will help Indian businesses meet global regulations and strengthen trade ties.

articles.title: There are little signs of economy overheating: Saugata Bhattacharya
articles.summary: Falling crude oil prices might boost growth beyond central bank forecasts, potentially easing rate hike needs. However, supply chain disruptions and embedded input cost pressures require close monitoring. While financial conditions appear restrictive, strong foreign currency inflows from new deposit and borrowing schemes could influence future monetary policy, with the RBI balancing growth concerns against inflation projections.

While financial conditions appear restrictive, strong foreign currency inflows from new deposit and borrowing schemes could influence future monetary policy, with the RBI balancing growth concerns against inflation projections.

articles.title: Sena UBT Crisis: MP Nagesh Ashtikar confirms switch to Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena
articles.summary: Sena UBT MP Nagesh Patil Ashtikar has confirmed his move to Eknath Shinde's Shiv Sena, citing development funds and constituency work as reasons. This switch may impact the balance of power in the party and potentially lead to changes in government policies. The MP's decision is seen as a significant development in the ongoing Sena UBT crisis.
articles.raw_summary: Sena UBT MP Nagesh Patil Ashtikar confirmed his move to Eknath Shinde’s Shiv Sena, saying he had not decided until June 18 and cited development funds and constituency work as reasons for switching sides.

articles.title: ONGC is now a 'gas-and-oil' company as output shift accelerates toward natural gas
articles.summary: ONGC is shifting its focus towards natural gas production, which is expected to rise as new fields come online. This shift is driven by the lucrative pricing of gas in the global market. The change may have implications for India's energy policy.
articles.raw_summary: While oil production is likely to remain flat, gas output will rise as newer fields are put into production. With one of the best pricing in the world, gas is now a more lucrative portfolio, he said.

articles.title: Kirloskar Oil Engines wins 192 MW power order for AI data centre
articles.summary: Kirloskar Oil Engines Limited (KOEL) has won a 192 MW power systems order from HyperNext to support a large AI-enabled data centre project in India. The deployment includes 96 units of its Optiprime Dual Core systems, which will provide backup power for hyperscale cloud and AI infrastructure. The project is aimed at building resilient, energy-efficient digital infrastructure to meet rising demand from artificial intelligence and cloud computing workloads.

articles.title: World Bank, India discuss framework to assess land governance reforms across states

articles.title: Contempt Court Can't Test Legality Of Decision Taken In Compliance With Underlying Judicial Order: Delhi High Court
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has reiterated that the validity or correctness of an administrative decision taken in compliance with a court's direction cannot be examined in contempt proceedings. Justice Tejas Karia made the observation while disposing of a contempt petition filed by a college, which alleged wilful disobedience by the National Council for Teacher Education (NCTE) of earlier directions requiring it to decide the college's long-pending application for shifting its B.Ed. college premises.

articles.title: UP Invests ₹5,400 Crore In Green Energy Corridor-II To Support 22 GW Solar Ambitions
articles.summary: Uttar Pradesh is advancing as a renewable energy hub with a ₹5,400 crore investment in the Green Energy Corridor-II project. This initiative will enhance power transmission infrastructure to achieve 22,000 MW of solar capacity. The project focuses on Bundelkhand's solar potential and encourages rooftop solar growth, aiming for a sustainable energy ecosystem. articles.title: UK FTA calls for standards upgrade: Experts
articles.summary: Indian exporters must elevate product standards and meet UK regulations to fully leverage the upcoming India-UK free trade agreement, effective July 15. Experts urge the government to launch nationwide outreach programs, mirroring UK efforts, to equip domestic industries. This proactive approach will help businesses transform the agreement's potential into tangible commercial success, establishing them as dependable supply chain partners.

This proactive approach will help businesses transform the agreement's potential into tangible commercial success, establishing them as dependable supply chain partners.

articles.title: Lower oil prices open door for MPC to time policy normalisation: ICICI Research
articles.summary: Falling oil prices and easing geopolitical tensions have improved India's inflation outlook, giving the Monetary Policy Committee room to decide on policy normalization timing. While concerns about food and fuel prices were noted, underlying inflation remains stable. Domestic growth shows resilience, though export and rainfall risks persist. The MPC may lower inflation forecasts if oil prices remain subdued, potentially reducing near-term rate hike odds.
articles.raw_summary: Falling oil prices and easing geopolitical tensions have improved India's inflation outlook, giving the Monetary Policy Committee room to decide on policy normalization timing. While concerns about food and fuel prices were noted, underlying inflation remains stable. Domestic growth shows resilience, though export and rainfall risks persist. The MPC may lower inflation forecasts if oil prices remain subdued, potentially reducing near-term rate hike odds.

articles.title: India-UK trade deal to offer export benefits from day-one; steel issues addressed: Govt Sources
articles.summary: The landmark India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) will come into force from July 15, 2026, with the government working to ensure that customs notifications and related processes are put in place so that exporters can start availing concessions from day one, senior government sources told ANI.

articles.title: Oil slides below $80 as US-Iran peace deal eases supply fears
articles.summary: The US and Iran have signed a peace framework, which has eased supply concerns and led to a drop in global oil prices. This may impact India's energy sector. The Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil shipping route, is expected to reopen.
articles.raw_summary: Brent slipped below $80 a barrel as the US and Iran signed a peace framework, easing supply concerns and paving the way for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Fuel curbs rollback likely as Iran deal raises hope
articles.summary: The Centre is considering lifting emergency curbs on fuel sales, which could ease pressure on farmers and lower inflation. This move comes after the US and Iran sued for peace. The decision could improve prospects for India, the world's third-largest oil importer.
articles.raw_summary: The Centre is weighing a rollback of emergency curbs on fuel sales imposed during the West Asia conflict after the US and Iran sued for peace, a move that could ease pressure on farmers, lower inflation and improve prospects for the world's third-largest oil importer.

articles.title: Net direct tax collections rise 14.6% to  ₹5.21 trillion as of 17 June
articles.summary: Net direct tax collections in India rose 14.6% to ₹5.21 trillion as of 17 June, driven by a 22.5% surge in corporate tax receipts and a near-45% jump in STT collections. This increase is significant for the Indian economy, but it does not directly imply any new policy or action.
articles.raw_summary: Net direct tax collections rose 14.64% to ₹5.21 trillion as of 17 June, led by a 22.5% surge in corporate tax receipts and a near-45% jump in STT collections.

articles.title: Basmati rice exports to West Asia poised for rebound after US, Iran sign peace deal
articles.summary: The reopening the Strait of Hormuz is expected to mitigate shipping bottlenecks, boosting trade to the Gulf, which represents a major market for Indian basmati rice.
articles.raw_summary: The reopening the Strait of Hormuz is expected to mitigate shipping bottlenecks, boosting trade to the Gulf, which represents a major market for Indian basmati rice.

articles.title: As war pressures ebb, edible oil prices may ease 10% ahead of the festive season
articles.summary: India could see edible oil prices fall by up to 10% in coming months as easing West Asia tensions, lower freight costs and higher palm oil supplies from Indonesia and Malaysia improve availability. However, benefits may take time to reach consumers due to existing inventories and supply contracts.
articles.raw_summary: India could see edible oil prices fall by up to 10% in coming months as easing West Asia tensions, lower freight costs and higher palm oil supplies from Indonesia and Malaysia improve availability. However, benefits may take time to reach consumers due to existing inventories and supply contracts.

articles.title: With Iran-US peace deal in sight, IEA projects oil supply surplus in 2027
articles.summary: The International Energy Agency (IEA) projects a global oil supply surplus in 2027, following a disruption in West Asia. This may impact India's energy policy. The IEA expects a 3.9 million barrels per day (bpd) decrease in oil supply this year, followed by an 8 million bpd rebound next year.
articles.raw_summary: Global crude oil supply is set to fall by 3.9 million barrels per day (bpd) to 102.4 million bpd this year amid trade disruptions due to the West Asia war, before rebounding by 8 million bpd next year, the International Energy Agency (IEA) said on Wednesday.

articles.title: Crude price fall may not last as nations rush to rebuild reserves
articles.summary: Global crude oil prices may not plummet due to a supply shortfall, despite a potential US-Iran peace deal. This could have implications for India's energy policy. The article highlights the complex factors influencing global oil prices.
articles.raw_summary: Even with a US-Iran peace deal on the horizon, global crude oil prices are not expected to plummet due to a significant supply shortfall. nbsp;

articles.title: States' liabilities rise to  ₹90.5 tn in FY25, many breach fiscal gap limit: CAG
articles.summary: As of 31 March 2025, the total liabilities of states stood at ₹90.51 trillion, while outstanding public debt reached ₹75.52 trillion. This reflects growing borrowing requirements across states, with public debt more than tripling over the past decade.
articles.raw_summary: As of 31 March 2025, total liabilities of states stood at ₹90.51 trillion, while outstanding public debt reached ₹75.52 trillion. Public debt has more than tripled over the past decade, rising from ₹23.92 trillion in 2015-16, reflecting growing borrowing requirements across states.

articles.title: Shipping lines stay cautious even after US-Iran peace deal, await security clarity before resuming Gulf operations
articles.summary: Shipping lines are cautious about resuming operations in the Gulf due to ongoing maritime conflict. Major lines like Maersk and Mitsui O.S.K. are waiting for security assurances before resuming operations. The conflict has led to increased costs for shipping companies.
articles.raw_summary: The US-Iran peace agreement raises hopes for smoother shipping in the Gulf, yet major lines Maersk and Mitsui O.S.K. remain hesitant. The industry awaits essential security assurances before resuming operations as costs surge due to the ongoing maritime conflict.

articles.title: Oil continues to trade subdued; market awaits peace deal details, reopening of Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Crude oil prices remain low due to a US-Iran peace framework. The Strait of Hormuz reopening uncertainty keeps markets cautious. This may impact India's energy sector and trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: Crude prices hovered near three-month lows after the US and Iran agreed on a peace framework, though uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz reopening kept markets cautious.

articles.title: Two rebel Shiv Sena MPs confirm exit — Omprakash Raje & Nagesh Patil switch over to Eknath Shinde's Sena
articles.summary: Two rebel Shiv Sena MPs, Omprakash Raje and Nagesh Patil, have switched over to Eknath Shinde's Sena. This move is significant as it affects the balance of power in the party. The decision may have implications for the party's future and its relationship with the government.

articles.title: Congress makes pitch for making right to vote a Fundamental Right in light of ‘partisan EC, SIR’
articles.summary: The Congress party has called for making the right to vote a Fundamental Right, citing concerns about the Election Commission's impartiality. This move is seen as a response to perceived biases in the Commission's functioning. The party's leader, Jairam Ramesh, pointed to Sardar Patel's views on universal adult franchise as a basis for this demand.
articles.raw_summary: 'Sardar Patel himself took the position that universal adult franchise was, in itself, an implicit fundamental right,' said Congress leader Jairam Ramesh.

articles.summary: Uddhav Thackeray has declared his faction as the sole Shiv Sena, countering Union home minister Amit Shah's statement. Thackeray accused the BJP of attempting to dismantle his party and poach its leaders, contrasting this with the Congress, with whom his party fought for decades but was never targeted for destruction. This comes after the Election Commission recognized Eknath Shinde's group as the official Shiv Sena.

articles.title: PE firms, mid-market companies drive India’s new GCC wave
articles.summary: India's Global Capability Centre (GCC) landscape is shifting, with private equity-backed and mid-market firms now driving significant expansion. These companies are leveraging GCCs beyond cost savings to build advanced capabilities in AI and engineering, creating substantial value for their portfolios. This trend signifies a maturing strategy for PE firms, moving GCCs from experimental to standard value-creation tools, with technology and financial services sectors leading the charge.

articles.title: ED case against five Bengaluru-based entities for 'unauthorised' cross-border crypto transactions
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate has initiated a FEMA probe into five companies located in Bengaluru for their alleged involvement in unauthorized cross-border transactions amounting to Rs 2,500 crore through cryptocurrency. Investigations uncovered significant regulatory evasion via online ads promoting immediate fiat-to-crypto swaps and the establishment of shell companies. Consequently, the ED has frozen bank accounts totaling around Rs 6 crore.

articles.title: India, US to hold ministerial-level talks on bilateral trade pact this week
articles.summary: In a significant development, India and the US are set to conclude the first phase of their trade agreement this week. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal is scheduled to meet US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer, with discussions geared towards finalizing an interim deal before the July 24 deadline, coinciding with the expiration of US temporary tariffs.
articles.raw_summary: In a significant development, India and the US are set to conclude the first phase of their trade agreement this week. Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal is scheduled to meet US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer, with discussions geared towards finalizing an interim deal before the July 24 deadline, coinciding with the expiration of US temporary tariffs.

articles.title: Exports rise 15 per cent in Apr-Jun 14 despite global uncertainties, says Piyush Goyal
articles.summary: India's merchandise exports surged approximately 15% in April-June, defying global economic headwinds, according to Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal. He highlighted robust growth despite significant US tariffs, with May exports reaching a six-month high. Goyal also urged chartered accountants to contribute to a 'Viksit Bharat,' emphasizing local cleanliness and ethical business practices as foundational steps.

He highlighted robust growth despite significant US tariffs, with May exports reaching a six-month high. Goyal also urged chartered accountants to contribute to a 'Viksit Bharat,' emphasizing local cleanliness and ethical business practices as foundational steps.

articles.title: RBI expected to hold repo at 5.25% as uncertainty eases, will watch food and fuel pass-through: BofA Securities
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is expected to maintain its wait-and-watch stance and keep the repo rate at 5.25 per cent in coming meetings as the US-Iran peace agreement reduces geopolitical uncertainty, while it monitors food and fuel price pass-through before acting, BofA Securities said in a note on the June MPC minutes.
articles.raw_summary: The Reserve Bank of India is expected to maintain its wait-and-watch stance and keep the repo rate at 5.25 per cent in coming meetings as the US-Iran peace agreement reduces geopolitical uncertainty, while it monitors food and fuel price pass-through before acting, BofA Securities said in a note on the June MPC minutes.

articles.title: Weak start to monsoon seen clouding Kharif season outlook: Report
articles.summary: India's monsoon has begun with a significant rainfall deficit, impacting kharif sowing, particularly for pulses and cotton. While reservoir levels offer a buffer, a prolonged dry spell could raise food prices and strain rural economies. Experts emphasize close monitoring, as the July-August period will be crucial for crop prospects and overall economic recovery.
articles.raw_summary: India's monsoon has begun with a significant rainfall deficit, impacting kharif sowing, particularly for pulses and cotton. While reservoir levels offer a buffer, a prolonged dry spell could raise food prices and strain rural economies. Experts emphasize close monitoring, as the July-August period will be crucial for crop prospects and overall economic recovery.

articles.title: India on track for $1 trillion exports in FY27: Piyush Goyal
articles.summary: India is poised to hit its ambitious $1 trillion export goal by FY27, despite ongoing West Asia challenges, according to Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal. He expressed strong confidence, citing progress and the impact of free trade agreements and improved business ease. Discussions with the US on a trade pact are finalized, awaiting India's tariff advantage over rivals for implementation.

Discussions with the US on a trade pact are finalized, awaiting India's tariff advantage over rivals for implementation.

articles.title: India, US trade deal can't be implemented until we have 'competitive advantage': Piyush Goyal
articles.summary: Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal on Saturday said India cannot implement the proposed trade agreement with the United States until it secures a competitive advantage over competing nations, indicating that tariff-related issues remain the final hurdle in the negotiations.
articles.raw_summary: Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal on Saturday said India cannot implement the proposed trade agreement with the United States until it secures a competitive advantage over competing nations, indicating that tariff-related issues remain the final hurdle in the negotiations.

articles.title: El Nino Impact: Milk prices likely to go up, again
articles.summary: El Nino is expected to lead to poor rainfall, which may cause farmers to reduce cattle numbers and lower milk output, potentially pushing up milk prices.
articles.raw_summary: Poor rainfall typically leads farmers to reduce cattle numbers because of shortages of fodder and water, lowering milk output and pushing up prices.

articles.title: Rs 19,700-crore carbon capture scheme gets nod
articles.summary: The carbon capture, utilisation and storage (CCUS) scheme is expected to attract investments of about ₹37,500 crore and create carbon-capture capacity of 7 million tonnes a year, they said. The next step is to get the approval of the Union Cabinet.
articles.raw_summary: The carbon capture, utilisation and storage (CCUS) scheme is expected to attract investments of about ₹37,500 crore and create carbon-capture capacity of 7 million tonnes a year, they said. The next step is to get the approval of the Union Cabinet.

articles.title: PLI, duty incentives soon for Lithium, Nickel processing units in India
articles.summary: The proposed scheme, which is close to securing approvals, will offer production-linked incentives and waive import duties on capital goods needed to establish processing facilities, a senior government official told ET.
articles.raw_summary: The proposed scheme, which is close to securing approvals, will offer production-linked incentives and waive import duties on capital goods needed to establish processing facilities, a senior government official told ET.

articles.title: India’s first export of fresh Uttarakhand Litchi to Italy marks entry into European market
articles.summary: Uttarakhand's famed Dehradun litchis have officially entered the European market with the first export consignment reaching Italy. This significant move, facilitated by APEDA, saw one metric tonne of the sweet, aromatic fruit dispatched on June 18, 2026. The initiative promises better returns for local farmers, who are now seeing prices around 25% higher than domestic rates, boosting the region's agricultural economy.

articles.title: India, Uzbekistan aim to double bilateral trade in 3 years
articles.summary: India and Uzbekistan are set to significantly boost bilateral trade, aiming to double it within three years. Discussions at the recent Intergovernmental Commission focused on expanding exports of pharmaceuticals, medical devices, auto, and machinery. Both nations also explored cooperation in customs, payment infrastructure, agriculture, ICT, and energy, with a particular emphasis on securing critical minerals for India's digital economy.

articles.title: Seven-fold rise in funds under externally aided projects in northeast under Modi government: Nirmala Sitharaman
articles.summary: Chairing a conference on "Leveraging externally aided projects in the northeastern states" here, she said that assistance under the externally aided projects (EAP) in the northeastern region has seen a seven-fold rise under the Narendra Modi government.
articles.raw_summary: Chairing a conference on "Leveraging externally aided projects in the northeastern states" here, she said that assistance under the externally aided projects (EAP) in the northeastern region has seen a seven-fold rise under the Narendra Modi government.

articles.title: RBI MPC member Nagesh Kumar flags import dependence, Gulf remittances, El Nino risks in minutes
articles.summary: India faces significant economic risks from the West Asia conflict, including potential disruptions to oil and fertilizer imports via the Strait of Hormuz and reduced remittances from the Gulf. El Niño-induced monsoon failures also pose a threat to agriculture. Despite these vulnerabilities, the nation's strong foreign exchange reserves and fiscal space offer some resilience. Growth projections have been tempered, highlighting the need for careful economic management.

articles.title: India’s forex reserves fall $9.99 billion to $671.63 billion as of June 12
articles.summary: India's foreign exchange reserves saw a dip of nearly $10 billion to $671.63 billion in the week ending June 12, 2026. This decline was primarily driven by a significant drop in gold reserves, despite a rise in foreign currency assets. The nation still holds a substantial forex buffer, crucial for economic stability and currency support amidst market fluctuations.

The nation still holds a substantial forex buffer, crucial for economic stability and currency support amidst market fluctuations.

articles.title: Ample world inventories may soften El Nino food supply shock
articles.summary: A potential super El Niño may disrupt global weather patterns and affect agricultural output. Large existing food inventories and strong harvests in key producing regions may soften the impact. This could have implications for India's food security and agricultural policies.
articles.raw_summary: A potential super El Niño is expected to disrupt global weather patterns and threaten agricultural output in the coming months, but large existing food inventories and strong harvests in key producing regions may soften the impact.

articles.title: Despite West Asia crisis, net direct tax mopup grows 15% to Rs 5.2 lakh crore
articles.summary: India's direct tax collections have seen a significant rise of over 14 percent. This growth is driven by strong advance tax payments from corporations. This indicates robust corporate earnings and a positive business outlook for the current fiscal year. Market activity also remains high, contributing to tax revenues. The collections have resumed their growth path, overcoming previous year's disruptions.
articles.raw_summary: India's direct tax collections have seen a significant rise of over 14 percent. This growth is driven by strong advance tax payments from corporations. This indicates robust corporate earnings and a positive business outlook for the current fiscal year. Market activity also remains high, contributing to tax revenues. The collections have resumed their growth path, overcoming previous year's disruptions.

articles.title: EPF interest rate retained at 8.25% for FY26
articles.summary: The EPF interest rate for 2025-26 has been set at 8.25%. This rate remains unchanged for the third year. Over 70 million EPF members will see the interest credited by month-end.
articles.raw_summary: Employees' Provident Fund interest rate for 2025-26 is set at 8.25 percent. This rate remains unchanged for the third year. Over 70 million EPF members will see the interest credited by month-end. EPFO is also introducing UPI-based withdrawals for faster access to funds.

articles.title: Peace in West Asia expected to boost order book of Indian exporters: Industry body FIEO
articles.summary: The Federation of Indian Export Organisations (FIEO) said that the conflict in West Asia suppressed India's trade volume, with exports to the Middle East suffering a sharp decline during the peak of the disruptions.
articles.raw_summary: The Federation of Indian Export Organisations (FIEO) said that the conflict in West Asia suppressed India's trade volume, with exports to the Middle East suffering a sharp decline during the peak of the disruptions.

articles.title: India's net direct tax collections rise 14.6% to Rs 5.21 lakh crore till June 17
articles.summary: India's direct tax collections have seen a significant jump. Net collections reached Rs 5.21 lakh crore by June 17, marking a 14.64% increase. This growth is fueled by strong corporate and non-corporate tax receipts. Advance tax payments also show a healthy upward trend, indicating robust income and profitability for taxpayers this financial year.

This growth is fueled by strong corporate and non-corporate tax receipts. Advance tax payments also show a healthy upward trend, indicating robust income and profitability for taxpayers this financial year.

articles.title: Will continue to support leveraging trade pact provisions, says UK India Business Council
articles.summary: India and the UK are set to implement their Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement on July 15. The UK India Business Council will assist its members in understanding and utilizing the pact's benefits. This landmark deal aims to boost trade and investment between the two nations. Focus areas include digital trade, services, manufacturing, education, and innovation.

Focus areas include digital trade, services, manufacturing, education, and innovation.

articles.title: 'Fastest we've ever put a trade deal into force': British High Commissioner to India Lindy Cameron hails India-UK trade deal
articles.summary: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA) will take effect on July 15, 2026, marking the fastest trade deal implementation between the two nations. This historic agreement aims to double bilateral trade to $100-$120 billion by 2030, offering significant economic benefits across sectors like textiles, footwear, and cars.

articles.title: India weighs extending 40-product import duty exemption beyond June 30 amid West Asia tensions
articles.summary: The government will decide on extending import duty exemptions for 40 products after June 30. This decision hinges on the evolving situation in West Asia and its impact on cargo movement. Revenue implications for the government are also a key consideration. The current exemption was put in place to support domestic industries facing supply chain disruptions.

The current exemption was put in place to support domestic industries facing supply chain disruptions.

articles.title: India to allow import of 3.78 lakh UK cars at concessional duty in first 15 years of trade pact
articles.summary: India will allow imports of up to 3.78 lakh conventional-engine passenger cars from the UK at concessional duties under a new trade pact starting July 15. Import duties will fall from 110% to 10% with quotas, while Indian electric and hybrid cars will gain duty-free access to the UK market from the sixth year.
articles.raw_summary: India will allow imports of up to 3.78 lakh conventional-engine passenger cars from the UK at concessional duties under a new trade pact starting July 15. Import duties will fall from 110% to 10% with quotas, while Indian electric and hybrid cars will gain duty-free access to the UK market from the sixth year.

articles.title: El Nino fear pushes farmers in Srikakulam and Vizianagaram towards water-saving paddy methods
articles.summary: Farmers in Andhra Pradesh's Srikakulam and Vizianagaram districts are adopting water-saving rice farming techniques to mitigate the effects of potential El Nino. This shift aims to reduce water usage and labor costs. The Dr Reddy's Foundation is promoting these changes to ensure sustainable agriculture in the region.
articles.raw_summary: Farmers in Andhra Pradesh's Srikakulam and Vizianagaram districts are shifting to water-saving rice farming techniques. Facing potential El Nino impacts, cultivators are embracing Direct Seeded Rice and Alternate Wetting and Drying. These methods significantly reduce water usage and labor costs. The Dr Reddy's Foundation is actively promoting these changes, alongside soil health improvements, to ensure sustainable agriculture in the region.

articles.title: UKIBC hails India-UK trade pact rollout from July 15, says CETA to unlock new growth opportunities
articles.summary: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement, CETA, will become effective on July 15, 2026. This landmark deal opens new avenues for trade and investment between India and the United Kingdom. Businesses are encouraged to prepare for its implementation. The agreement is set to boost economic engagement and create employment opportunities in both nations.
articles.raw_summary: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement, CETA, will become effective on July 15, 2026. This landmark deal opens new avenues for trade and investment between India and the United Kingdom. Businesses are encouraged to prepare for its implementation. The agreement is set to boost economic engagement and create employment opportunities in both nations.

articles.title: Govt ratifies 8.25% EPF interest rate for FY'26, to be credited this month
articles.summary: The government has approved an 8.25% interest rate on Employees' Provident Fund (EPF) deposits for FY26, clearing the proposal recommended by the Employees' Provident Fund Organisation's (EPFO) Central Board of Trustees in March. The interest is expected to be credited to the accounts of over seven crore EPF subscribers later this month.

The interest is expected to be credited to the accounts of over seven crore EPF subscribers later this month.

articles.title: India taps World Bank, ADB for $2.5 billion infrastructure spend
articles.summary: India is in talks with the World Bank and Asian Development Bank for substantial funding. This financial support aims to boost urban infrastructure and create jobs. The loans will help New Delhi manage its budget after increased spending on subsidies. The government plans to upgrade infrastructure and urban renewal projects.

articles.title: Restoring India’s land, securing India’s future through science and collective action
articles.summary: Scientific innovations and integrated farming systems are combating land degradation and enhancing climate resilience, benefiting millions of farmers and contributing to national development goals.

articles.title: Stocks in news: Sun Pharma, Tech Mahindra, Tata Motors, Voltas, ONGC
articles.summary: Indian markets saw a dip on Friday, with IT stocks leading the decline. Analysts suggest profit-taking occurred near the 24,150 mark. Despite this, the broader recovery trend remains intact above 23,700. Several companies like Sun Pharma, Tata Motors, and ONGC are in focus due to significant business developments, including acquisitions, order wins, and strategic repositioning.

articles.title: Can Jio and NSE IPOs repeat Maruti feat?
articles.summary: US and Indian IPO markets are gearing up for major listings, with AI giants like OpenAI and Anthropic set to capitalize on investor frenzy. In contrast, India's anticipated Jio and NSE IPOs face a less enthusiastic market after years of stagnant returns. While US IPOs may signal market tops, India's offerings are expected to be more sober, potentially reigniting foreign investor interest.

While US IPOs may signal market tops, India's offerings are expected to be more sober, potentially reigniting foreign investor interest.

articles.title: Carlyle eyes $1 billion raise via auto, healthcare IPOs
articles.summary: Private equity giant Carlyle is gearing up for significant IPOs in India, planning a $500 million listing for its automotive platform, Highway Roop Precision Technologies, valued at an estimated $2 billion. Concurrently, its healthcare RCM platform is also set for a $400-500 million offering. These major moves are anticipated by mid-2027, signaling substantial growth for Carlyle's Indian ventures.

articles.title: NHB probing Aavas Financiers over loan classification lapses
articles.summary: The sector regulator has recalled refinancing support worth nearly Rs 500 crore —a punitive action that has set off a sweeping leadership overhaul at the company. ET was the first to report on April 13 that managing director and CEO Sachinder Bhinder was being asked to step down, with Manu Singh — former home loans head at Kotak Mahindra Bank — set to take over. A week later, on April 20, the company confirmed Bhinder's resignation and Singh's appointment as the new CEO.

A week later, on April 20, the company confirmed Bhinder's resignation and Singh's appointment as the new CEO.

articles.title: Regulatory minefield: ED enters the crypt, waking up the ghosts for Crypto Inc
articles.summary: India's crypto sector faces a new crisis as the Enforcement Directorate probes four firms for unauthorized foreign money transfers using digital coins. This action highlights potential breaches of foreign exchange laws, even when the underlying funds are legitimate. The move could significantly impact crypto exchanges' ability to source offshore liquidity, raising concerns about cross-border virtual asset transactions and their compliance with FEMA regulations.

The move could significantly impact crypto exchanges' ability to source offshore liquidity, raising concerns about cross-border virtual asset transactions and their compliance with FEMA regulations.

articles.title: Indian banks turn cautious on MSME lending as stress signals emerge: Report
articles.summary: Indian banks are becoming more cautious about lending to MSMEs as early signs of stress emerge in the sector. According to a 360 ONE Capital report citing CRIF High Mark data, MSME loan growth slowed to 12.7% year-on-year in April 2026 from around 18%-20% in previous quarters, while growth in active loans decelerated sharply to 2.5%.
articles.raw_summary: Indian banks are becoming more cautious about lending to MSMEs as early signs of stress emerge in the sector. articles.title: Bringing back open-market buybacks through stock exchanges is the right decision
articles.summary: SEBI’s decision to restore open-market buybacks through stock exchanges marks a pragmatic regulatory shift that supports efficient capital allocation. The move recognises evolving market dynamics while maintaining safeguards, giving companies greater flexibility to return surplus capital and strengthening confidence in market-led decision-making.

articles.title: Father’s Day: The financial legacy children truly inherit
articles.summary: Indian fathers are redefining legacy, moving beyond just assets to financial behaviour. The rise of micro-investing and digital platforms allows for consistent, small-scale participation, making wealth creation an everyday habit. This shift, driven by accessibility and automation, teaches children valuable lessons in discipline and long-term planning, shaping a more enduring inheritance than mere wealth.

articles.title: Earnings of OMCs seen weak as Q1FY27 under-recoveries bite: Report
articles.summary: Oil companies in India are expected to face challenges due to under-recoveries in Q1FY27. This is likely to affect their profit margins. The government's possible retraction of excise duty cuts could further exacerbate the situation.
articles.raw_summary: Oil companies are projected to experience ongoing challenges through FY27, primarily due to anticipated under-recoveries in Q1FY27, with LPG losses being a considerable issue. Although recent declines in crude prices provide short-term relief, ongoing market volatility and required inventory adjustments are expected to squeeze profit margins. A notable threat includes the government's possible retraction of excise duty cuts to mitigate revenue shortfalls.

articles.title: Dividends & bonus issues: LIC, Asian Paints among 35 stocks turning ex-record date this week. How many do you own?
articles.summary: Around 35 companies, including LIC and Asian Paints, have fixed record dates for dividends, bonus issues, and buybacks. Investors must hold shares before the record date to qualify for these corporate actions. This may impact the stock market and investor decisions.
articles.raw_summary: Around 35 companies, including LIC, HUL, Asian Paints and IndusInd Bank, have fixed record dates for dividends, bonus issues and buybacks in the upcoming week. Investors must hold shares before the record date to qualify for corporate actions, though additional announcements may follow.

articles.title: The yoga of asset allocation: Why stability matters more than ever
articles.summary: Investors need stability in today's volatile markets. Asset allocation, including bonds, can help mitigate sharp market movements and ensure long-term wealth creation. This is crucial for investors looking to grow their wealth.
articles.raw_summary: In today's volatile markets, investors need stability more than ever, drawing parallels from yoga's emphasis on balance. Asset allocation, particularly the inclusion of bonds, is crucial for mitigating sharp market movements and ensuring long-term wealth creation. While equities offer growth, bonds provide predictable returns and act as a stabilizing force, helping portfolios withstand global shocks and domestic surprises.

articles.title: Warsh's gamble: A quieter Federal Reserve could mean volatile markets, higher rates
articles.summary: The new Federal Reserve Chair, Kevin Warsh, is reducing the central bank's communication, which may lead to market volatility and higher borrowing costs. This change could have implications for the global economy, including India. Analysts warn that this move may contrast with previous chairs' efforts to stabilize markets and lower rates.
articles.raw_summary: New Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh is significantly reducing the central bank's communication, particularly "forward guidance" on interest rates. This move, aimed at making markets less reliant on Fed signals, has already caused market volatility. Analysts warn this could lead to higher borrowing costs for consumers and businesses, contrasting with previous chairs who used guidance to stabilize markets and lower rates.

articles.title: Jio Platforms plans $3 billion debt reduction from IPO proceeds
articles.summary: Jio Platforms plans to use a portion of its IPO proceeds to repay nearly $3 billion in external commercial borrowings. This move aims to reduce debt and interest expenses, allowing the company to pursue strategic growth in areas like 5G, broadband, AI, and cloud services.
articles.raw_summary: Jio Platforms is gearing up for its IPO, planning to allocate a significant portion of the funds to repay nearly $3 billion in external commercial borrowings for its telecom arm. This move aims to slash debt, reduce interest expenses, and bolster the company's financial standing. The deleveraging will empower Jio to pursue strategic growth in areas like 5G, broadband, AI, and cloud services, positioning it for future investments and opportunities.

articles.title: Monsoon risk, younger investor base, and skewed trading activity define NSE outlook: NSE data
articles.summary: India's 2026 macro outlook will hinge on monsoon performance, while the investor base continues to get younger and more geographically dispersed, and trading activity remains heavily concentrated among large investors, NSE data showed in its latest report.
articles.raw_summary: India's 2026 macro outlook will hinge on monsoon performance, while the investor base continues to get younger and more geographically dispersed, and trading activity remains heavily concentrated among large investors, NSE data showed in its latest report.

articles.summary: BSE subsidiary ICCL has adopted a new brand identity as BSE Clearing, marking a significant milestone in its alignment with parent entity BSE Ltd. This move reinforces its role within the broader BSE ecosystem. The transition is a regulatory action that strengthens the alignment between the two entities.
articles.raw_summary: The company said the transition marks a significant milestone in strengthening alignment with parent entity BSE Ltd and reinforces its role within the broader BSE ecosystem.

articles.title: Free shares! NSE IPO DRHP reveals curious case of 5,000 shares landing in wrong demat account
articles.summary: According to the DRHP, NSE and Nuvama Wealth Finance filed a civil suit before the Delhi High Court against Kashmiri Lal Rana and NSDL in May 2025, alleging that 5,000 NSE shares were mistakenly transferred to Rana's demat account on December 28, 2023, despite no corresponding purchase request or consideration being paid.

articles.title: NSE IPO: Nithin Kamath explains why India has few businesses like this ‘cash generating machine’
articles.summary: In a post on X, formerly Twitter, Kamath described NSE as a "cash generation and distribution machine", noting that the exchange earned more than Rs 10,300 crore in profit in FY26 and distributed about Rs 8,660 crore as dividends, translating into a payout ratio of 84%.

articles.title: Jio IPO: Spectrum acquisition, among 7 risks investors need to know about India’s largest offer
articles.summary: The IPO comes at a time when Jio's operating performance remains robust. For the March quarter of FY26, the telecom giant reported a 13% year-on-year increase in operating revenue to Rs 44,928 crore, while net profit rose 13% to Rs 7,935 crore. EBITDA grew 18%, aided by a 230 basis-point expansion in operating margins.

articles.title: Mohandas Pai backs SEBI's buyback revival, says battered markets are ideal for creating shareholder value
articles.summary: Welcoming SEBI's decision to restore open-market buybacks, Mohandas Pai said Indian companies holding large cash reserves should use market corrections to repurchase undervalued shares, while Equirus' Bhavesh Shah expects more firms to opt for the exchange route due to its greater flexibility.

articles.title: TN Assembly unanimously adopts resolution against Karnataka's Mekedatu dam, opposition parties back government
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu Assembly unanimously adopted a resolution against Karnataka's Mekedatu dam. Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay piloted the resolution, which was backed by opposition parties including Leader of the Opposition Udhayanidhi Stalin and AIADMK chief Edappadi K Palaniswami. The resolution is a significant development in the nearly 13-year-long dispute between the two states.

articles.title: Tamil Nadu finally admits its biggest debt problem. But can it afford the solution?
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu's latest fiscal White Paper identifies the power sector as the state's biggest source of financial risk, with electricity utilities carrying debt of ₹2.47 lakh crore and accumulated losses of ₹1.82 lakh crore. While recent assessments suggest the financial health of the state's distribution utility has improved, the key question is whether the turnaround is sustainable—or whether the burden has simply shifted from TNEB's balance sheet to the state's own finances.

articles.summary: Macquarie Capital's Suresh Ganapathy believes that private banks are a better investment choice due to strong credit growth in India. This is because the valuation gap between private and public sector banks is now in favor of large private lenders. The financial sector is expected to remain positive, but the rally in public sector banks may be nearing its end.
articles.raw_summary: While maintaining a positive view on the broader financial sector, Suresh Ganapathy, Managing Director and Head of Financial Services Research, Macquarie Capital, believes valuation gaps between private and public sector banks now favour large private lenders, making them the preferred investment choice for the coming year.

articles.title: Central banks around the globe expect gold reserves to increase
articles.summary: Central banks around the world, including those in emerging markets, plan to increase their gold reserves. articles.raw_summary: World Gold Council survey shows 45% of 74 central banks plan to boost gold reserves, mainly in emerging markets, despite recent price pullback and rising political risk

The government assures continued efforts to protect India's maritime and energy interests, with the vessels expected to reach Indian ports next week.

Meanwhile, the nation's equity investor base is rapidly expanding, becoming younger and more diverse, with a notable surge in participation from smaller cities. Despite this growth, trading activity remains heavily concentrated among a small group of large investors across all market segments.

Major dairies are closely monitoring the situation.

articles.title: A look back at Shiv Sena's 60-year political journey: Bal Thackeray's rise, Uddhav takes over and then a split
articles.summary: Shiv Sena, a political party in India, is facing internal turmoil as six MPs from Uddhav Thackeray's camp seek a split. This development may impact the party's future and potentially lead to changes in Indian politics. The party's 60-year history has seen several rebellions, and this latest development adds to the uncertainty.

articles.title: A stock trader’s guide to navigating a rare ‘Super El Niño’
articles.summary: Investors are reassessing their holdings due to the potential impact of a Super El Niño on the agriculture and energy sectors. This could lead to inflationary risks and challenges for central banks. The situation is being closely watched by market participants.
articles.raw_summary: Investors are reassessing holdings as climate risks rise with a possible Super El Niño impacting agriculture and energy sectors. The situation poses inflationary risks and challenges for central banks, complicating the current equity market environment.

articles.title: Mint Explainer| What would it take for India to create a SpaceX?
articles.summary: SpaceX’s $2.5 trillion valuation is driven by its reusable rocket technology, which slashed launch costs and dominates the global space market. India’s startups, Skyroot and Agnikul, are targeting small-satellite launches, but need capital, scale and consistent execution to build a success story.

articles.title: Mint Explainer: Big Tech seeks to sell cheaper AI. Can India benefit?
articles.summary: Big Tech is investing billions to revolutionize AI by drastically reducing training costs and enhancing deployment efficiency. As Google and Microsoft shift their focus to selling AI at scale, how is India positioning itself in this rapidly evolving landscape?

articles.title: Mint Explainer | Can connected cars help India cut road fatalities?
articles.summary: Auto industry and tech companies point out that since a vast majority of road accidents are caused by human error, such vehicle connectivity technologies can provide proactive, pre-crash interventions to mitigate these issues.

articles.title: Summer Solstice 2026: What is the longest day of the year and why does it happen?
articles.summary: In India, the summer solstice does not usually bring the hottest temperatures, as many parts of the country are already influenced by the southwest monsoon by late June.
